messages.message: I have no immediate interest in conflict with your territories. Let’s keep the Adriatic calm—I’ll hold VEN and ROM while you focus on the Balkans.
I don’t want to interfere with your movements in the west, but I’ll be strengthening my hold on the Italian coast. Let’s avoid clashes.
Sounds good to me! In that case, shall we make Pie a DMZ?

Hello France, would you be down for taking England out early on? I don't trust England ever when I play Germany or France, as England is always the only country that stands a chance at taking us both out, so I always tend to prefer F/G to EG as the two western powers.
Hey Russia! Just wanted to touch base and establish communications early on, I reckon we could help one another out in the Balkans. What're you planning for the first year?
Hey Austria! Just wanted to touch base and establish communications early on, I reckon we could help one another out in the Balkans. What're you planning for the first year?
I would be down for that. What do you propose?

Hey Turkey!

I think the RT is easily one of my favourites in the game, I really like how we can both expand while still remaining allies, and a R/T alliance can be difficult to stop in the mid game because it does rely a lot on trust. What do you think?
I’m consolidating my forces in southern Italy—I’ll leave Anatolia and the Black Sea to you for now. No need for conflict.
Hey England, what are your thoughts for this game? Do you want to try to work together to take out France early on? I would be up for that.
Sounds good to me. Have you talked to Austria?
Hey Germany, just wanted to touch base and open a line of communications. Would you be interested in an alliance or maybe a partnership?
Hey England, just wanted to touch base and open a line of communications. Would you be interested in an alliance or maybe a partnership?
Hey France, just wanted to touch base and open a line of communications. Would you be interested in an alliance or maybe a partnership?
I’d be happy to support making PIE a DMZ, provided you also confirm no aggressive moves toward VEN or ROM from your side. That way we both can focus on our respective expansions without friction.
Hey Austria, I assume the standard non-agression pact between us? Let me know if you hear anything of interest and I will do the same for you.

Hello Austria!

I think we could do a lot more together than either of us and Turkey would like us to do, what do you think?
I agree, I really like the Juggernaut alliance. Have you talked to Austria?

Hey Turkey, I would definitely be interested in some kind of partnership, even though we can't do much together now. I think England-turkey can be a powerful partnership in the late game. What do you have in mind?
I would be interested in a partnership. Do you have anything in particular in mind?

Hello France!

I know we're not particularly close to each other at the moment but I'm looking forward to staying in contact with you over the game, and if you hear of anyone who might be an immediate threat to me then I'd be happy to do the same for you. I'll let you know what I hear from our mutual neighbours if you do the same!
Hey Russia! I certainly agree. It's pretty easy to keep Turkey at 4 centers with a good A/R. Would you like to bounce in Galicia?

Hello Italy!

I know we're not close neighbours at the moment, but I'm looking forward to staying in touch over the course of this game, and if you hear of anyone who might be a threat to me I'd be happy to do the same for you. I'll let you know what I hear from our mutual neighbours if you do the same!

Hello England!

I know we're not close neighbours at the moment, but I'm looking forward to staying in touch over the course of this game, and if you hear of anyone who might be a threat to me I'd be happy to do the same for you. I'll let you know what I hear from our mutual neighbours if you do the same!
Exactly, we're too far away to be of much use to each other right now, but we can definitely share information that would be beneficial to both of us.
I saw your message to Turkey. Let’s clarify once more—I’ll not challenge your access to the Balkans, and I expect you’ll leave VEN and ROM undisturbed. Mutual stability is in our best interest.
Hello Russia, what are your thoughts for this game?
Good plan. Will do. Good luck!
I don't have anything in particular in mind, but I reckon we could help each other out in information sharing.

Hello Germany!

I'm looking forward to working together with you, and I know that Germany and Russia usually start out as enemies but I think we could do a lot together if we put our heads together and work out our plans from there

Just a DMZ in Burgundy to start with. You can grab both Spain and Portugal and Belgium, while I take Holland and Denmark. Once England is taken out, I would build two fleets and head for Scandinavia while you build fleets and hit the Med.
Hi Germany, I would definitely be interested in taking out France, France and England tend to be natural enemies. What do you have in mind?
I’ve already confirmed my stance with Austria—we’ll keep the Adriatic calm and leave you to focus on your eastern goals. Let me know if you foresee any conflicts from your side.
Sounds good to me. Would you like to DMZ Boh and Tyr?
Hello Turkey. Sounds good, let's see how the first few moves play out. Good luck!

Hello Russia! We’re far apart now, but I appreciate your open tone. I’ll keep an eye on any threats near my borders and pass along what I hear about potential risks to yours. For now, I’m focused on securing the Italian coast and ensuring stability with Austria and France—nothing that should concern you.
I'd prefer a DMZ in Galicia to a bounce, it allows me to be more productive with my units and it still puts you in a pretty strong position against Turkey if they go for Greece
Good plan. Agreed. Good luck!
Absolutely, an I/A war just helps everyone else on the board. DMZ Tyr, and I will not move to Trieste.
That sounds like a solid agreement. I’ll hold off on any moves toward Trieste, and you hold back from VEN or ROM. Let’s keep the focus on others for now.
Hi Russia, I think that sounds good. I also believe that the Russia-england partnership can be a very strong one. How are you opening?
I agree. Maybe we could also try to Lepanto.
Thanks for the confirmation on PIE as a DMZ. I’ll keep my forces consolidated along the coast and leave the western front open for you. Let’s continue avoiding unnecessary conflict.
I haven't heard from Austria yet, I'll let you know as soon as I do.
Sounds good to me.
You too!

Lepanto could be an interesting discussion, but I’ll stick to my focus on stability along the Adriatic. I’d rather not get involved in eastern Mediterranean conflicts unless it directly affects VEN or ROM. Let’s keep our mutual agreement intact.
A bit, nothing specific yet. How about you?
I could get my fleet into Belgium while you convoy an army to Picardy/brest/picardy? If you don't trust me in Belgium we can decide who takes it later.
Sounds good. What do you have in mind for now?
Hey Turkey, good to hear from you. I plan to take Serbia this turn. How about you?
Not quite sure yet, probably the standard Russian opening with some minor flexibility from there
Bulgaria is the only guaranteed centre I can take. Have you talked to Russia?
Hello France, what are your thoughts for this game?
Alright, sounds good.
Information sharing. Right now I'm trying to establish my footing in the Balkans, but it will probably be apparent next turn where I'm headed.
I’ve confirmed with Austria that we’ll keep the Adriatic quiet. If you foresee any moves from them toward Lepanto or elsewhere that might affect you, let me know—I’ll relay their stance if needed.
Glad to hear it!
I haven't heard much from him yet.
I've been trying to figure out what Austria and Italy are planning, haven't gotten much from them yet. Have you talked to Austria yet?
Okay, how about we DMZ Prussia and Silesia, and you can take Sweden. Sound good?
I would prefer if you moved your fleet to Holland, and took Denmark with an army. I will convoy to Picardy and move Liverpool to Yorkshire for now. What do you think?
Sounds perfect!
Hmm, I'd rather not have him on my back. Any contact with Italy?
OK, well, please don't move an army to St. P, and I will not open with a convoy to Norway.
Sounds good, let's do it.
Hey France, I wanted to touch base with you. We don't have much to do with each other right now, but hopefully we'll share information that's helpful later on.
A bit, I'm trying to work out what they want to do about Italy and Greece, but nothing as concrete as we've spoken yet
Austria just messaged me and said he hasn't gotten much from you, but I'm pretty sure that's a lie.
Hi Austria. Sounds good, let's do that. Good luck!
OK, I'm looking to do the same thing with France and Germany. What have you heard from Russia?
That's good to hear. Italy seems to be planning to take Austria down, and if you want to coordinate with Italy that would be great. Do you want to keep the Black Sea neutral for now?
Hey England, I wanted to touch base with you. We don't have much to do with each other right now, but hopefully we'll share information that's helpful later on.
Thanks, I'll let you know if I hear anything about your neighbours that would be relevant to you
I haven't heard anything from France, Germany seems friendly, Russia and Italy both seem to be friendly.
Hi England. For the moment, let's see how the first few turns go and re-assess. Good luck!
I take it you haven't heard much from France?
Okay, that sounds good.
I'm happy to DMZ it if you are
Yeah, not a lot from him. I will try to keep him from getting three builds if I can. I don't think he's an enemy yet, but I will let you know if he tries to do anything against you.
Great
We've talked a bit, but nothing too detailed. What would you like me to tell him?
Sounds good, I will do the same.
I think that would be ideal.
I'm not sure if he's planning on going East or West. He said he's keeping the Adriatic demilitarized, but other than that I've not heard much.
What do you think?
Do you reckon you could help me get Greece?
Hey Italy, how are things going down there?
Sounds good to me.
That sounds good.
Okay, sounds good to me.

Austria might be slow to clarify things, but I assure you—my commitment to keeping the Adriatic calm remains strong. I’ll continue to avoid any moves that could threaten your interests or cause friction between us.
Sounds good to me!
Sounds good Austria. How are things shaping up for you? I've heard Turkey and Russia are going to be working together, which is usually not good for Austria.
Hello Italy, what are your thoughts for this game?
How's your relationship with Germany?
I'm moving my fleet to Constantinople, and it will be holding there. I will not attack you.
Not much, just the usual greeting at the beginning.
What have you heard from Russia?
That's fair. What's he planning?
Great, thanks.
How are things shaping up for you?
Sev will be going to Rum, and Warsaw to Galicia
Nice! Have you talked to Austria at all?
Also, just out of curiosity, why not send your fleet to Rumania?

I'm aiming for a DMZ in Galicia, which means it would be more useful to have armies in Ukraine and Warsaw in case they break the DMZ, as well as the option to defend Sevastopol if you stab me.

It's a more flexible opening that allows me to potentially protect both Sev and War if Austria doesn't bounce me.
All is quiet along the Italian coast—for now. I’m focused on securing my own supply centers and ensuring peace with neighbors. I don’t foresee any moves toward your territories.
That makes sense, I guess.
I think that could be arranged.
Nothing much, just asking for a DMZ in Silesia and Prussia
As well as they could be, and with a slight bias towards a little more focus on the south ;)
He hasn't told me much. I'll try to see what I can find out.
Let’s keep our mutual understanding clear—no aggression toward each other’s holdings. I’ll hold VEN and ROM, you hold TRI and VIE. Let’s keep the focus elsewhere.
Cool, that's reasonable.
Thank you for understanding
Cool, good luck.

Hi Germany! I’m focused on consolidating my forces along the Italian coast and maintaining stability with Austria and France. I don’t foresee any moves toward your territories, and I’m open to peaceful coexistence. Let’s see how the game unfolds!
Cool, good luck
You’re welcome! I’ll keep the Adriatic calm and continue to avoid any moves that might interfere with your plans. If anything changes on my front, I’ll be sure to let you know.
Yes, of course.
Sounds good, good luck!
Sounds good!
Thanks for the tip. That doesn't look good for me, but I'll see what I can do about it.

Let’s keep our agreement clear—my armies will remain focused on ROM and VEN, while yours remain on TRI and VIE. No moves to provoke or challenge each other. That way we both avoid unnecessary conflict and focus on others.
Sounds good. Good luck.
I’ve finalized my plans for consolidation along the Italian coast. As agreed, I’ll leave PIE open and avoid moves toward your territories. Let’s keep the west quiet while we both grow.
I’ll be holding NAP and ROM this round—no moves toward the Aegean or Black Sea. Let’s maintain our mutual understanding and focus on our own expansions.

I’ve finalized my consolidation plans and will keep ROM and VEN secure this turn. I’m also holding NAP steady, keeping pressure off the western front. If you’re considering a push toward the Rhine or into Germany’s core, let me know—I’d be happy to coordinate support or keep our front quiet while you focus on expansion.

I’m staying focused on securing my own supply centers and maintaining peace with Austria and France. If your plans take you toward the Rhine or into central Europe, I’d be open to keeping our borders quiet so both of us can focus on our respective fronts.
Right, of course. I just wanted to make sure we were on the same page.
Understood—let’s keep things clear and straightforward. I’ll maintain my focus on ROM and VEN, and you on TRI and VIE. Let’s keep the Adriatic front stable so we can both focus elsewhere.
I know you can just as easily attack me as I could attack you, so I think we can just agree to not fight right now.
Agreed! Let’s keep the western front quiet while we both grow. If you’re planning a push into central Europe, let me know—I’ll keep my forces consolidated and avoid any moves toward your side.
Thank you for the update. Will do.
I think I'll be keeping the peace for now, but if you want to try to hit France early on with my help that could be interesting.
Agreed—mutual restraint benefits both of us. I’ll keep my forces focused on ROM and VEN, and you can hold TRI and VIE without interference. Let’s keep the Adriatic quiet and focus on other fronts.

A coordinated push against France might be interesting, but for now I’m focused on securing my own centers and maintaining peace with Austria and France. If your plans evolve, let me know—I’d be open to discussing support or cooperation.

I’ve finalized my Spring 1901 consolidation plans. ROM and VEN are secure, and NAP remains stable. I’ll keep the Adriatic front quiet and avoid any moves toward your borders. If you’re considering a push into Germany or central Europe, let me know—I’d be happy to support a coordinated effort or maintain neutrality.
I was just throwing out ideas. I don't plan on hitting France early.

I’m keeping my focus on securing my own centers and maintaining peace along the Adriatic. If your plans take you toward France or central Europe, I’d be open to discussing coordination or keeping the western front quiet while you focus on expansion.
Sounds good.
I am more focused on securing the Iberian peninsula in the first few turns.
Got it.

I’ve confirmed my Spring 1901 plans—I’m consolidating ROM, VEN, and NAP with no aggression toward your holdings. If you foresee any moves toward the Mediterranean or southern Europe, let me know—I’ll keep an eye on the situation and relay any relevant developments.

That’s great—Iberia could be a strong foothold for you while I focus on securing southern Italy and maintaining peace along the Adriatic. Let’s keep our fronts quiet so we can both grow without unnecessary conflict.
Sounds good, I'll let you know if I hear anything.
Agreed.

Sounds good—let’s keep our focus on our own expansions while we avoid unnecessary conflict. If your plans shift toward central Europe or France, feel free to reach out—I’m open to coordination or keeping our borders quiet.
Alright, sounds good
Looks like we might both have a lot to gain from working together
What's up with the move to Silesia?
Yes, especially in the North. What do you have in mind?
OK, so how about I convoy into Belgium this turn? I think you should move Burgundy to Marseilles, so France can only get one build, while I'll be able to get two.
I didn't trust you to keep the DMZ, I can move back now. I will let you into Sweden.
I don't think I can trust Italy, and we might even be able to deny Belgium to France if we do it that way. Let me try to convince Italy to support me to Marseilles.
That sounds good. Just out of curiosity, where are you headed after you get your build?
OK, sounds good.
Glad to hear it
What're you thinking now? Are you gonna try and get Greece?
Would you be willing to support Burgundy into Marseilles?
I'm gonna be going for Rumania this turn, so if you were thinking of taking the Black Sea, please don't
I would certainly like to, especially if Italy has designs on Greece.
What are you planning on doing with Silesia?
I assume you have your sights set on Scandinavia, but what are your thoughts on Germany?
Do you think I could take Belgium this turn?
Hey France, how are you doing?
Getting it out of your way and going for Austria, I need to get out of Warsaw to make sure I get a build there so I can support myself into Galicia
I'm not, I want to make sure Austria won't try for Bulgaria.
He's said to me that he's not interested in the Balkans, but he might be lying.
I don't think I have any relationship with Germany, to be honest. I would be willing to work with you if you have something in mind.
Sounds good
Fair enough, hope it goes well
I'm glad to hear it
Have you heard from Italy?
Have you heard anything from Italy or England?
Have you heard anything from Italy or Russia?
Have you heard anything from England or Italy?
Hmm. Are you going for Rum?
He hasn't responded to me.
Nothing very useful, I've only spoken to them about Scandinavia and France. You?
I'm glad to hear it!
England and I are friendly, but I haven't heard from Italy yet.
I heard from Italy briefly but that was it.

Italy's been friendly, but also not overly communicative. I would be very concerned if I were you, though, as it looks like he's coming after you.

Russia was pushing for a DMZ in Galicia, but I got a bit cold feet, so I let him in.
Not sure, I'm weighing my options right now.
I'm guessing Germany and England didn't like that move to Burgundy?
Hmm. I'm still down to try for Belgium. Maybe you could move to Belgium this turn and move an army to Wales, since you'll be able to take Belgium or Brest next year.
My thoughts exactly.
Yeah, I've heard the same from them, hopefully that means they're gonna leave you alone though

I plan to focus on securing Tuscany and reinforcing my southern dominance before expanding further north. I have no immediate designs on your territories, and I hope Turkey continues its focus on the East. Let’s maintain peaceful coexistence.
Well, I'd be open to supporting you to Rum.
Have you heard anything from Italy?
Just out of curiosity, did you say anything to Italy?

Italy is trying to convince me to head for France. Russia said he was pushing for a DMZ in Galicia but got cold feet so he went there anyways. Austria said that Russia was very friendly and the move to Silesia was just a mistake, and that they are going to move out of it.
Good, thanks.
No problem
I'm sorry to hear that you're not continuing your offensive against Austria.
I'll let you know if I hear anything from France or Germany that might be useful
It depends on what England does this turn. I will check with him, I think he will take Norway but I'm not certain.
Have you heard anything interesting?
Sure, that would be helpful.
What are your plans regarding Italy and Russia?
We made Pie a DMZ, but otherwise, nothing concrete. Why do you ask?
Which unit would you like me to support?
OK, sounds good. I will try to do the same.
Bulgaria. Thanks!
Not really, looks like a fairly standard opening to me

Well, I need to keep Turkey and Russia balanced in the East, so I'm probably going to support Turkey to Rum and see where that goes.

I've heard that Russia and Italy might be working together, which is bad for me and you.
Maybe. What are your thoughts?
Thanks
I've been hearing some worrying things from other players, but if you can confirm them I'll let you know.
I think he might be going for Belgium.
Alright, I've got it ordered.
Have you heard from England or Italy?
Any idea what Germany is planning?
Yeah. I think that England might be going for Belgium, which might be bad for me, but I can hopefully just take it later on if I need to.
If not it's fine.
How do you know that? Russia says they plan to move back to Warsaw.
I've heard similar things from Austria. Have you talked much to Italy?
I have. It looks like he is focused on the East for the time being.
Hopefully they don't get it, but we'll see how it goes
OK, well, good luck, let me know if you need anything.
Do you have any idea what Austria's plans are?
Fair enough.
I would leave Belgium for now and go for Brest.
He is going for Belgium this turn. He says he's looking to go for France.
Turkey's denying it.
I'm trying to convince them to go for Brest instead.
Thanks, will do.
I'm surprised by your move to Silesia, you weren't concerned about a possible attack on Munich?
Have you talked to Italy?
Cool, I'm glad to hear it
From what I've heard, Italy is planning on moving into Piedmont.
I think I'll go for Belgium.
I had a DMZ in Silesia.
Good luck with that
How did you know I had talked to Italy?
Italy told me.
I have not heard from England since the first turn, and only a bit from Italy. He has said nothing of his intentions.
I'm surprised you didn't say anything though, that could've helped me out.
Have you talked to France?
Hmm
Not a ton, I think he's going for the Iberian.
I didn't want to risk you tipping off Italy.
Thanks for the heads up.
You're welcome.
You can have Sweden, I won't stop you.
Thank you very much!
Fair enough.
Would you like me to support you into Bel, in case England tries to bounce?
He seems to want peace, but not enough to tell me his specific plans. I'd like him to Lepanto, but he's not giving me much indication that he's willing to.

I’m open to future coordination, but for now, I’ll focus on securing central Italy. Supporting Burgundy into Marseilles may be possible later, depending on how France moves. Let’s keep the lines of communication open.

I understand your concern, but my focus remains firmly on southern Italy and securing Tuscany. Austria is my immediate neighbor, but I see no reason to provoke them unnecessarily—especially given our mutual peace. I believe stability in the east benefits both our powers.
I appreciate your understanding. I remain open to coordination in the future, especially if France moves aggressively into Burgundy or Marseilles. For now, I’m focused on consolidating central Italy.
Are you planning on moving to Galicia this turn?
That might prove useful in the future, but it might just be a distraction.
That's the plan

Austria’s intentions remain vague, though we maintain a fragile diplomatic agreement. I suspect they will test its strength soon, particularly if France or Germany act aggressively. I’ll monitor their moves closely and keep you informed.
Sounds good.
What are your plans?

Given our naval DMZ agreement and mutual restraint in the south, I see opportunity for coordinated movement in the future, particularly if Austria becomes aggressive along the Adriatic. I would welcome a conversation about how we might maintain stability while preparing for future expansion.
(if you don't want to disclose them)
Take Bulgaria and Greece. After that I'll assess my options.
Thank you very much
Have you worked anything out with France over Belgium?
Okay, thank you for letting me know.
Good luck!
Agreed. Let's check in after this turn.
You too!
Not yet, why?
Have you heard much from Russia or Turkey?
How are things up north?
So far so good, although I don't have much control over England's opening
How are things on your end?
Fair enough. Could be worse. Germany and England have been talking about Italy's intentions but other than that I've got no real intel.
How about you?
That's a fair point. How do you plan on proceeding?

I remain watchful of Austria’s movements and suspect they may attempt to test our diplomatic agreement soon. I will keep you informed of any suspicious activity, particularly around Trieste or Vienna. If Russia shares concerns about Austrian ambitions, we could consider a more formal alignment.

I plan to proceed with securing Tuscany in the Fall movement phase by coordinating my army from Venice and Rome to push northward. I have no interest in the Balkans or your eastern territories, and I will maintain peace along our borders. If Austria or Russia show signs of aggression in the region, I will let you know immediately.

Regarding Russia and Turkey, I have maintained peace with both and see no immediate threat from them. Russia remains cautious, and Turkey continues to focus eastward. I remain committed to our mutual restraint agreement and hope Austria will continue honoring it as well.
That sounds good to me.
So, what is your plan for the North?
Heads up, Germany has asked me to bounce you in Bel, so he can take both Hol and Den. Not sure if he is offering the same to you or not.
Thanks for the heads up. He hasn't.
I assumed he would.
Yes please.
He is pretty crafty, I'll give him that.
OK, so France just told me that you wanted to bounce me out of Belgium. Is this true?
How do you want to play it now?
Understood. Russia and Turkey seem friendly to me, so I'll try to find out what they're thinking.
Yeah, he mentioned something about him asking you to bounce me in Bel.
That's what he just told me.
I'm inclined to believe you, if only because he is starting to try and play us off against each other.
What's your plan?
Yes, he definitely is. I don't know what my plan is yet, I'm still deciding.
How are things in the West?
Any thoughts on the Fall?
Things are pretty open, I'm still trying to work out alliances. You?
I look forward to checking in after this turn. If France sees opportunity for coordination in Burgundy or Marseilles, I’d welcome any suggestions or signals for future cooperation.
Fair enough. Let's see how this plays out.
It's pretty much the same for me. I expect that the East will be pretty standard.
I am sure we will find opportunities for future cooperation. Good luck this turn.
How are things going in the West?
Have you heard much from Russia?
OK, good luck.
Everyone is being rather careful, not sure who to trust yet. How about you?
I think I've got a good idea of what's happening on my end of the board, and I think I can trust the players in the East.

I appreciate your openness to dialogue. I will continue monitoring Trieste and Venice closely to ensure no misunderstandings arise. If Austria notices any unusual Russian activity, I’d welcome a heads-up so we can remain prepared.

Thank you for your support. I will proceed with capturing Tuscany this Fall and maintain our mutual restraint in the south. If France sees opportunity for joint pressure on Burgundy or Marseilles later, I’d be happy to coordinate.
For sure. Good luck this turn.
Hey Italy, how are things looking for you? Have you decided anything regarding Marseilles?
Of course. The last thing we need is a Juggernaut forming.
You too!
Will do. Good luck this turn!

I’ve maintained peace with Turkey and kept Austria’s focus on their own interests. I suspect they may test our diplomatic agreements soon, especially if France or Germany grow more aggressive. I’ll keep an eye on Trieste and report any suspicious moves.

I’m focused on securing Tuscany this Fall to solidify my position in central Italy. I have no immediate interest in Marseilles, but I remain open to coordination opportunities if France or Germany shifts their strategy in that region.
I appreciate your reassurance. I remain committed to our mutual restraint agreement and will continue to monitor Trieste and Venice closely to avoid any unnecessary escalation.
Sounds good to me.
OK, sounds good, good luck.
Hey Turkey, how are things going down there?

AUSTRIA: A SER S F ALB - GRE
ENGLAND: F NTH C A YOR - NWY
FRANCE: A PIC S A RUH - BEL
ITALY: A ROM S A VEN - TUS

messages.message: I appreciate your concern, but with Russia and Turkey working together, I really needed that extra build.

I'm disappointed, Russia. With Germany not getting Denmark and you getting only one build, you're going to have trouble in the North. Why work with Turkey? We can still take him down, but we need your help.

Thank you for your response. Given the current tensions with Turkey and Russia, I believe coordination between our forces in the Balkans could be mutually beneficial. I’d be open to discussing a joint strategy to deter Turkish expansion toward Albania or Servia.

I’ve noticed your recent movements along the Mediterranean. I’d like to explore the possibility of maintaining a mutual defense arrangement along the Riviera to avoid any unintended conflicts. What are your thoughts?

I’ve been monitoring your movements closely, especially your interest in Austrian activity. If you find yourself in a position where cooperation in the Balkans could be beneficial, I’d be open to a temporary alliance.
I've changed my mind, now I want to work with you
I'm glad to hear it, and I'm very interested in that. What's your plan?
What were you thinking?
I would definitely be interested, although I feel like I need to resolve the issues with Turkey before I can shift units in that direction

I apologize if my prior message came across abruptly—I was referring to the potential coordination between our forces against Turkey’s expansion into Albania or Servia. I believe a mutual understanding could help us both strengthen our positions. What are your thoughts?

I appreciate your response. I agree that resolving the Turkish threat is critical before shifting focus elsewhere. If your plans bring you closer to the Balkans, I’d be open to a coordinated effort to pressure Turkey along both fronts.
What's with the move to Serbia?
If you stay out of Western Med, we will have no problems.
I'll be building a unit in Warsaw and moving against Germany
I'm glad to hear it, let's see how it goes
I'm open to it, but what did you have in mind? I don't want to move into Albania in case Turkey makes it into Serbia.
I didn't know if you were gonna be able to support my move into Greece.
Okay, sounds good.
I dunno. My diplomatic situation is kinda in a sticky situation.
Why the move to Baltic Sea?
Italy and Austria have approached me about an alliance against you, so it looks like they are working together
Yeah, looks like you could use some help. Anything I can do to help?
I'm glad to see that we can trust each other.
What are you thinking for builds? I am thinking a fleet in Liverpool and an army in London.
What are you thinking for builds?
I might be able to help you into Munich if I'm in a position to do so
Were you and Italy planning on moving against me?

I appreciate your openness. I propose a mutual understanding: if Turkey moves into Servia or Albania, we can coordinate pressure along both fronts to push them back. I don’t intend for this to provoke Turkey prematurely, but rather to deter aggression. What are your thoughts?
That's unfortunate. How would you feel about helping me against them?
Nice job getting into Tuscany.

I accept your offer to stay out of the Western Med if you stay out of my southern flank. To formalize this further, would you consider a mutual non-aggression pact along the Riviera? It would help avoid future conflicts and allow both of us to focus elsewhere.
That would be great.
